item.title: 1.4 Billion Credentials Found on Dark-Net
item.description: Researchers have found a 41GB data file which contains 1.4 billion user credentials in plain text which passes the 711 million accounts of the Onliner…

item.title: Microsoft Releases Malware Patch for its…Malware Protection Engine
item.description: Yesterday, Microsoft released a patch to fix a remote code execution flaw, CVE-2017-11937, in its Malware Protection Engine (MPE).

item.title: Over 5,000 WordPress Sites Infected with Keylogger
item.description: Researchers have recently seen that more that 5,000 WordPress websites have been infected with a malware that was seen earlier this year.

item.title: Binary Defense in Gartner EDR Market Guide
item.description: Binary Defense is proud to be included in Gartner's most recent Market Guide for Endpoint Detection and Response Solutions.

item.title: LockCrypt Ransomware
item.description: Attackers have been recently breaking into corporate servers via RDP brute force attacks to spread a new variant of ransomware dubbed “LockCrypt”

item.title: New Vulnerability Uses Antivirus Software to Inject Malware
item.description: Multiple antivirus programs have been found to have a vulnerability that allows the “restore from quarantine” feature to be used for malicious purposes.

item.title: MantisTek GK2 Keyboard Sends Data to China
item.description: The Mantistek GK2 gaming keyboard is a popular keyboard in the gaming community, it has been seen that the keyboard has been silently recording key strokes

item.title: ,000 Stolen from Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Cryptocurrency wallets are currently being targeted by the CryptoShuffler Trojan. It is unclear who is behind the attack, however, $150,000 of Dash,…

item.title: Data Breaches During First Half of 2017 Exceed all of 2016
item.description: These numbers do not include last month’s Equifax and SEC breach announcements, or Yahoo’s revised report of its 2013 exposure.

item.title: Fake Cryptocurrency Apps Steal User Data
item.description: In August 2017, a security researcher was able to bypass Poloniex’s 2FA (two-factor authentication) by finding a Reddit thread dubbed “Poloniex2FASucks.”

item.title: New "Reaper" Malware has Infected Two Million IoT Devices
item.description: A newly-discovered malware is rapidly spreading and is thought to have already infected over two million IoT devices. It was dubbed “Reaper_IoT”

item.title: DoubleLocker Android Ransomware
item.description: A new ransomware has surfaced in the wild that’s targeting Android devices dubbed as DoubleLocker.

item.title: Hackers Can Hack Kids' Smartwatches and Track Locations
item.description: Smartwatches designed for kids are helping parents ensure the safety of their children by being able to keep track of them via secure apps.

item.title: KRACK Attack WPA Vulnerabilities
item.description: Researchers have discovered and documented critical weaknesses in WPA2, a protocol that secures all modern protected Wi-Fi networks

item.title: The Certainty of Uncertainty...Why Cyber Security Resiliency Matters
item.description: Today's hyper-crisis world demands a better way to ensure security success. Any size organization needs to be an adaptive security enterprise.

item.title: Vision EDR Platform Disrupts Another Phishing Attack, Sorry Bad Guys
item.description: Binary Defense Vision EDR can easily detect macro malware leveraging Windows PowerShell and, in this case, helped a customer defend against the Emotet…

item.title: Announcing Vision 4.0 Platform – Managed EDR Combined with EPP
item.description: Introducing Vision 4.0 Platform Managed Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) with built-in Endpoint Platform Protection (EPP) by Binary Defense.

item.title: Social Engineering Attacks and Mitigations Part VII: Whaling
item.description: Phishing is the most common type of social engineering attack that has targeted companies for years. Phishing attacks get sent out to a mass amount of…

item.title: Social Engineering Attacks and Mitigations Part V: Phishing
item.description: How do companies protect themselves from phishing email attacks in the modern age?

item.title: Social Engineering Attacks and Mitigations
item.description: Social Engineering is a security term that may be thrown around in corporate offices numerous times but does everyone at your company know what it means?

item.description: Last week, I mentioned the assassination of a Chinese shipping executive who was killed in his car while parked near an upscale market in Karachi,…

item.title: EvilOSX
item.description: On February 14th, 2018, a new variant of an OS X RAT called “EvilOSX” has appeared on Github. A RAT (Remote Access Trojan) is a type of malware

item.description: Over the past year, there have been a number of attackers sending threats to major organizations pretending to be members of these various groups…

item.description: It is no doubt that social media has a hold on people around the world, but it has an especially mesmerizing effect on the factory workers of China’s…

item.title: Fake Online FBI Scam
item.description: A new cyberattack is impersonating the FBI’s Internet Crime Complaint Center and trying to scam personal data from victims or download malicious files

item.title: Massive Botnet Turns Windows Machines into Miners
item.description: Researchers have discovered a massive botnet that has taken over half a million Windows devices and turned them into cryptocurrency miners, Smominru

item.title: Digmine Virus Spreads Via Facebook Messenger
item.description: A new cryptocurrency mining virus has risen, this time the virus is spreading through Facebook’s messenger app and is targeting Google Chrome desktop…

item.title: Google AdWords and Google Sites Used to Spread Malware
item.description: This particular scam will trick the victim into downloading a fake Google Chrome installer however, an infected file installs “Potentially Unwanted…

item.description: This year has shown us some of the biggest cyber threats in history, with millions of consumers and businesses having been hit by the WannaCry attack, and…
